title: Interactive pH Scale Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Explore a color-coded pH scale with real-world pins while converting between pH, pOH, [H+], and [OH-] values in real time.

learningObjectives: Relate positions on the pH scale to typical acidic/basic substances.
Convert between pH, pOH, [H+], and [OH-] using the logarithmic definitions.

keyQuestions: Where do common substances fall on the pH scale?
How does changing pH affect [H+] and [OH-] concentrations?
commonMisconceptions: A change of one pH unit doubles acidity (instead of ×10).
assessmentOpportunities: Students record computed values for assigned pH points.
Learners explain why seawater is basic even though it is close to neutral.
